title: Orchestrator PlanAuditor Integration
description: Validates that Orchestrator correctly routes plans to PlanAuditor for adversarial review based on trigger conditions

inputs.label: complex-plan-triggers-challenger
inputs.description: A 5-phase plan should trigger PlanAuditor review

inputs.label: low-confidence-triggers-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase plan with confidence 0.7 should trigger PlanAuditor review

inputs.label: simple-plan-skips-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase plan with high confidence should skip PlanAuditor

inputs.label: destructive-plan-triggers-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase plan with destructive operations should trigger PlanAuditor review

inputs.label: plan-auditor-rejected-escalates
inputs.description: When PlanAuditor returns REJECTED, Orchestrator must escalate to user

inputs.label: plan-auditor-needs-revision-routes-to-planner
inputs.description: When PlanAuditor returns NEEDS_REVISION, Orchestrator routes back to Planner

inputs.label: semantic-risk-high-data-volume-triggers-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase, high-confidence, non-destructive plan with applicable HIGH data_volume risk must trigger PlanAuditor

inputs.label: semantic-risk-resolved-skips-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase, high-confidence plan with applicable HIGH data_volume risk that is resolved must skip PlanAuditor

assertions: PlanAuditor is invoked for plans with 3+ phases
PlanAuditor is invoked for plans with confidence < 0.9
PlanAuditor is invoked for plans with destructive operations
PlanAuditor is NOT invoked for simple plans (< 3 phases, high confidence, non-destructive, no unresolved HIGH-impact risk_review entries)
Orchestrator passes plan_path (not inline plan) to PlanAuditor
governance/runtime-policy.json remains the authoritative source for PLAN_REVIEW trigger thresholds and tier-specific iteration caps
NEEDS_REVISION routes back to Planner (max iterations per complexity tier: SMALL=2, MEDIUM/LARGE=5)
ABSTAIN from PlanAuditor does not block pipeline
PlanAuditor is invoked for plans with any risk_review entry applicability=applicable AND impact=HIGH AND disposition != resolved
When triggered by semantic risk, Orchestrator derives focus_areas from risk category mapping and includes them in delegation
PLAN_REVIEW reruns default to full rerun for sensitive or ambiguous changes, including prompts, policy surfaces, routing, commands, contracts, and orchestration handoff tests/scenarios
Selective rerun is allowed only for reviewer-local summary wording or evidence-citation text with no plan/prompt/policy/test/routing/command changes
Selective rerun never bypasses ExecutabilityVerifier when current tier or risk override keeps it in scope
